summary: DirectDine iter7 tested (backend + frontend). AuthContext.tsx correctly imports Platform + Notifications (lines 2-3). Backend: 11 new iter7 tests + 65 iter6 regression tests all pass (76/76). Haversine distance + in_range filtering + sort work correctly for both Beyoglu and Kadikoy coordinates. PUT /api/restaurants/me accepts delivery_radius_km and persists. Frontend: location-bar renders with 'Delivering to' text, distance chips shown (0.0 km for Bella Napoli from Beyoglu), 'Restaurants near you' + 'Show all (2)' toggle works and reveals out-of-range Sakura (5.8 km) & Smash (10.2 km) with opacity 0.6 + 'Out of delivery range' tag + red distance. Owner profile → Restaurant settings modal opens, prefilled with current radius (4), Save updates via PUT (verified via GET: 10.0). Reset back to 4.0 after test. Featured card also shows distance chip. Minor: DB contains 3 leftover restaurants from previous manual owner registrations (wwww, Test Rest, TEST Switched Diner) with default radius 5km — not a code bug but distorts 'expected 3 seeded' assertions; tests were made tolerant.

minor.issue: delivery_radius_km lacks Pydantic constraint (no gt=0). Values 0 and negative numbers are accepted silently and persist to DB. Non-numeric correctly rejected with 422. Recommend adding `Field(gt=0)` to RestaurantUpdate.delivery_radius_km and RestaurantCreate.

Preview DB still has 3 leftover test restaurants (wwww, Test Rest, TEST Switched Diner) from prior owner-registration test runs. Original problem statement claimed 'DB was fully wiped', but these persist. Not blocking — the 3 seed restaurants (Bella Napoli 4km, Smash 8km, Sakura 3km) are present with correct radii.

action_items: OPTIONAL: Add `Field(gt=0)` validator to RestaurantUpdate.delivery_radius_km (currently 0 and negative values silently pass through). Non-numeric already returns 422.

OPTIONAL: Consider a one-time cleanup script (or a smarter idempotent seed) to purge leftover test-created restaurants from the preview DB so the customer home isn't cluttered with 'wwww'/'Test Rest'/'TEST Switched Diner'.
critical_code_review_comments: AuthContext.tsx imports (Platform, Notifications) correctly added on lines 2-3 — iter6 defect fixed.

server.py list_restaurants: sort key `(not r.in_range, r.distance_km if r.distance_km is not None else 9e9)` is correct — in-range first, then by ascending distance. No location => distance_km stays None and list keeps DB order.
server.py update_my_restaurant uses `{k:v for k,v in data.dict().items() if v is not None}` — correct partial-update pattern.
home.tsx: out-of-range card onPress is guarded (`() => !isOut && router.push(...)`) — tapping is a true no-op as spec'd.
LocationContext.tsx persists loc via AsyncStorage with idempotent clear on null — clean.
server.py is 1159 lines — approaching the 700-line guideline threshold; consider splitting routes into routers by feature (restaurants / orders / auth / couriers) in a future iteration.
seed_data_creation: None. Test mutates Bella Napoli's delivery_radius_km temporarily (4→12.5→4 in pytest, 4→10→4 in Playwright) and restores it after each test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: iter7 backend tests live at /app/backend/tests/test_directdine_iter7.py. Login testIDs are `login-email-input`, `login-password-input`, `login-submit-button` (NOT the shorter versions). Owner profile is served at /profile route (not a tab labeled 'Profile' via get_by_text). Playwright must use context.grant_permissions(['geolocation']) + context.set_geolocation({...}) BEFORE navigation so LocationContext auto-loads. Preview DB currently has 3 leftover restaurants beyond the 3 seeded ones — write tolerant assertions.
rca_of_issue: N/A — no defects reproduced. iter6's missing-imports defect was independently verified fixed: /app/frontend/src/context/AuthContext.tsx lines 2-3 now contain `import { Platform } from 'react-native';` and `import * as Notifications from 'expo-notifications';`, and both are used in registerPushForUser (lines 32-40). registerPushForUser early-returns on web so no ReferenceError path can be hit in the preview.
